evals.prompt: Run a structural analysis of this codebase at /home/user/myproject. Generate the retro report.
evals.expected_output: Retro-engineer checks for tools/retro/main.py (or tools/software/discovery/main.py). If found, runs: python tools/software/discovery/main.py /home/user/myproject --output retro-report.md --json. Reads the generated retro-report.md and retro-report.json. Produces a summary covering: technology stack, module structure, entry points, external API calls, and hardware signals (if any). If hardware signals detected, dispatches to hardware-analyst.

assertions.text: Checks for and attempts to run the automated analysis tool before doing manual analysis
Output includes: technology stack, architecture map, entry points
If hardware APIs are detected, dispatches to hardware-analyst

evals.prompt: Analyze this project but the tools/retro/main.py script doesn't exist yet.
evals.expected_output: Falls back to manual analysis: uses Grep, Glob, and Read tools to perform the analysis steps manually. Covers: (1) language detection via file extensions; (2) entry points via main.py, __init__.py, setup.py patterns; (3) import graph via grep for import statements; (4) external API calls via grep for known library patterns. Documents findings in retro-report.md. Does NOT silently fail — proceeds with available tools and notes the missing automated tool.

assertions.text: Does not crash or refuse to work when main.py is absent
Performs manual analysis using available search tools
Notes the missing automated tool and suggests invoking tool-writer to create it
